title: cerastium lucorum subsp. macrocarpum
intro: Cerastium lucorum subsp. macrocarpum is a sub-species of the perennial herb Cerastium lucorum, commonly known as a flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, derived from its roots, leaves, and stems. The health benefits of Cerastium lucorum subsp. macrocarpum are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various skin conditions and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
